heading: 9.7.14.5.16.Warp-level matrix store instruction:stmatrix
content:
`stmatrix`

Collectively store one or more matrices to shared memory.

Syntax

```
stmatrix.sync.aligned.shape.num{.trans}{.ss}.type [p], r;

.shape  = {.m8n8, .m16n8};
.num    = {.x1, .x2, .x4};
.ss     = {.shared{::cta}};
.type   = {.b16, .b8};
```

Description

Collectively store one or more matrices across all threads in a warp to the location indicated by
the address operand `p`, in `.shared` state space. If no state space is provided, generic
addressing is used, such that the address in `p` points into `.shared` space. If the generic
address doesn’t fall in `.shared` state space, then the behavior is undefined.

The `.shape` qualifier indicates the dimensions of the matrices being loaded. Each matrix element
holds 16-bit or 8-bit data as indicated by the `.type` qualifier.

`.m16n8` shape is valid only for `.b8` type.

The values `.x1`, `.x2` and `.x4` for `.num` indicate one, two or four matrices
respectively.

The mandatory `.sync` qualifier indicates that `stmatrix` causes the executing thread to wait
until all threads in the warp execute the same `stmatrix` instruction before resuming execution.

The mandatory `.aligned` qualifier indicates that all threads in the warp must execute the same
`stmatrix` instruction. In conditionally executed code, an `stmatrix` instruction should only be
used if it is known that all threads in the warp evaluate the condition identically, otherwise the
behavior is undefined.

The behavior of `stmatrix` is undefined if all threads do not use the same qualifiers, or if any
thread in the warp has exited.

The source operand `r` is a brace-enclosed vector expression consisting of 1, 2, or 4 32-bit
registers as per the value of `.num`. Each component of the vector expression holds a fragment
from the corresponding matrix.

Supported addressing modes for `p` are described in [Addresses as Operands](#addresses-as-operands).

Consecutive instances of row need not be stored contiguously in memory. The eight addresses required
for each matrix are provided by eight threads, depending upon the value of `.num` as shown in the
following table. Each address corresponds to the start of a matrix row. Addresses addr0–addr7
correspond to the rows of the first matrix, addresses addr8–addr15 correspond to the rows of the
second matrix, and so on.

| `.num` | Threads 0–7 | Threads 8–15 | Threads 16–23 | Threads 24–31 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `.x1` | addr0–addr7 | – | – | – |
| `.x2` | addr0–addr7 | addr8–addr15 | – | – |
| `.x4` | addr0–addr7 | addr8–addr15 | addr16–addr23 | addr24–addr31 |

When storing 8x8 matrices, a group of four consecutive threads stores 16 bytes. The matrix addresses
must be naturally aligned accordingly.

Each thread in a warp stores fragments of a row, with thread 0 storing the first fragment from its
register `r`, and so on. A group of four threads stores an entire row of the matrix as shown in
[Figure 107](#mma-stmatrix-fragments).





[Figure: mma-stmatrix-fragments]
STMATRIX FRAGMENT LAYOUT: ONE 8x8 MATRIX WITH 16-BIT ELEMENTS

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
Matrix size: 8 rows x 8 columns
Element type: 16-bit elements (e.g., .f16 or .bf16)
This describes the stmatrix instruction's fragment layout for a single 8x8 tile.

2. GRID STRUCTURE
Rows: 8 matrix rows (labeled 0 through 7)
Columns: 8 matrix columns (labeled 0 through 7)
Total threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads)
Each cell in the table is labeled as "Tx:r" where x is the thread index and r is the register holding that element.

3. COMPLETE ELEMENT-TO-THREAD-REGISTER MAPPING (Row by Row)

Row 0: Col0=T0:r, Col1=T0:r, Col2=T1:r, Col3=T1:r, Col4=T2:r, Col5=T2:r, Col6=T3:r, Col7=T3:r
Row 1: Col0=T4:r, Col1=T4:r, Col2=T5:r, Col3=T5:r, Col4=T6:r, Col5=T6:r, Col6=T7:r, Col7=T7:r
Row 2: Col0=T8:r, Col1=T8:r, Col2=T9:r, Col3=T9:r, Col4=T10:r, Col5=T10:r, Col6=T11:r, Col7=T11:r
Row 3: Col0=T12:r, Col1=T12:r, Col2=T13:r, Col3=T13:r, Col4=T14:r, Col5=T14:r, Col6=T15:r, Col7=T15:r
Row 4: Col0=T16:r, Col1=T16:r, Col2=T17:r, Col3=T17:r, Col4=T18:r, Col5=T18:r, Col6=T19:r, Col7=T19:r
Row 5: Col0=T20:r, Col1=T20:r, Col2=T21:r, Col3=T21:r, Col4=T22:r, Col5=T22:r, Col6=T23:r, Col7=T23:r
Row 6: Col0=T24:r, Col1=T24:r, Col2=T25:r, Col3=T25:r, Col4=T26:r, Col5=T26:r, Col6=T27:r, Col7=T27:r
Row 7: Col0=T28:r, Col1=T28:r, Col2=T29:r, Col3=T29:r, Col4=T30:r, Col5=T30:r, Col6=T31:r, Col7=T31:r

4. DATA TYPE
16-bit elements per cell. Each register r holds two 16-bit values packed into one 32-bit register (since two consecutive columns in the same row map to the same thread and same register, e.g., Row0 Col0 and Col1 are both T0:r).

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
Each matrix row is covered by exactly 4 threads.
Row 0: T0, T1, T2, T3
Row 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11
Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15
Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19
Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23
Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27
Row 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

Each thread covers exactly 2 consecutive columns within its assigned row. Specifically:
Within each row, the 4 threads cover column pairs: T(base+0) covers cols 0-1, T(base+1) covers cols 2-3, T(base+2) covers cols 4-5, T(base+3) covers cols 6-7.

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
Each thread holds exactly one 32-bit register r for this 8x8 matrix.
That single register r contains two 16-bit elements packed together (the two elements from the two consecutive columns assigned to that thread in its assigned row).
For example, T0 holds register r containing the two 16-bit values at (Row0, Col0) and (Row0, Col1) packed into one 32-bit register.
This applies uniformly to all 32 threads T0 through T31.

SUMMARY TABLE OF THREAD ASSIGNMENTS:
Thread | Row | Cols    | Register
T0     |  0  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T1     |  0  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T2     |  0  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T3     |  0  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T4     |  1  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T5     |  1  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T6     |  1  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T7     |  1  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T8     |  2  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T9     |  2  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T10    |  2  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T11    |  2  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T12    |  3  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T13    |  3  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T14    |  3  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T15    |  3  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T16    |  4  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T17    |  4  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T18    |  4  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T19    |  4  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T20    |  5  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T21    |  5  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T22    |  5  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T23    |  5  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T24    |  6  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T25    |  6  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T26    |  6  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T27    |  6  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T28    |  7  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T29    |  7  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T30    |  7  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T31    |  7  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)

STMATRIX FRAGMENT LAYOUT: ONE 8x8 MATRIX WITH 16-BIT ELEMENTS

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
Matrix size: 8 rows x 8 columns
Element type: 16-bit elements (e.g., .f16 or .bf16)
This describes the stmatrix instruction's fragment layout for a single 8x8 tile.

2. GRID STRUCTURE
Rows: 8 matrix rows (labeled 0 through 7)
Columns: 8 matrix columns (labeled 0 through 7)
Total threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads)
Each cell in the table is labeled as "Tx:r" where x is the thread index and r is the register holding that element.

3. COMPLETE ELEMENT-TO-THREAD-REGISTER MAPPING (Row by Row)

Row 0: Col0=T0:r, Col1=T0:r, Col2=T1:r, Col3=T1:r, Col4=T2:r, Col5=T2:r, Col6=T3:r, Col7=T3:r
Row 1: Col0=T4:r, Col1=T4:r, Col2=T5:r, Col3=T5:r, Col4=T6:r, Col5=T6:r, Col6=T7:r, Col7=T7:r
Row 2: Col0=T8:r, Col1=T8:r, Col2=T9:r, Col3=T9:r, Col4=T10:r, Col5=T10:r, Col6=T11:r, Col7=T11:r
Row 3: Col0=T12:r, Col1=T12:r, Col2=T13:r, Col3=T13:r, Col4=T14:r, Col5=T14:r, Col6=T15:r, Col7=T15:r
Row 4: Col0=T16:r, Col1=T16:r, Col2=T17:r, Col3=T17:r, Col4=T18:r, Col5=T18:r, Col6=T19:r, Col7=T19:r
Row 5: Col0=T20:r, Col1=T20:r, Col2=T21:r, Col3=T21:r, Col4=T22:r, Col5=T22:r, Col6=T23:r, Col7=T23:r
Row 6: Col0=T24:r, Col1=T24:r, Col2=T25:r, Col3=T25:r, Col4=T26:r, Col5=T26:r, Col6=T27:r, Col7=T27:r
Row 7: Col0=T28:r, Col1=T28:r, Col2=T29:r, Col3=T29:r, Col4=T30:r, Col5=T30:r, Col6=T31:r, Col7=T31:r

4. DATA TYPE
16-bit elements per cell. Each register r holds two 16-bit values packed into one 32-bit register (since two consecutive columns in the same row map to the same thread and same register, e.g., Row0 Col0 and Col1 are both T0:r).

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
Each matrix row is covered by exactly 4 threads.
Row 0: T0, T1, T2, T3
Row 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11
Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15
Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19
Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23
Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27
Row 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

Each thread covers exactly 2 consecutive columns within its assigned row. Specifically:
Within each row, the 4 threads cover column pairs: T(base+0) covers cols 0-1, T(base+1) covers cols 2-3, T(base+2) covers cols 4-5, T(base+3) covers cols 6-7.

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
Each thread holds exactly one 32-bit register r for this 8x8 matrix.
That single register r contains two 16-bit elements packed together (the two elements from the two consecutive columns assigned to that thread in its assigned row).
For example, T0 holds register r containing the two 16-bit values at (Row0, Col0) and (Row0, Col1) packed into one 32-bit register.
This applies uniformly to all 32 threads T0 through T31.

SUMMARY TABLE OF THREAD ASSIGNMENTS:
Thread | Row | Cols    | Register
T0     |  0  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T1     |  0  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T2     |  0  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T3     |  0  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T4     |  1  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T5     |  1  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T6     |  1  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T7     |  1  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T8     |  2  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T9     |  2  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T10    |  2  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T11    |  2  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T12    |  3  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T13    |  3  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T14    |  3  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T15    |  3  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T16    |  4  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T17    |  4  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T18    |  4  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T19    |  4  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T20    |  5  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T21    |  5  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T22    |  5  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T23    |  5  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T24    |  6  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T25    |  6  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T26    |  6  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T27    |  6  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)
T28    |  7  | 0,1     | r (packed pair)
T29    |  7  | 2,3     | r (packed pair)
T30    |  7  | 4,5     | r (packed pair)
T31    |  7  | 6,7     | r (packed pair)


Figure 107 stmatrix fragment layout for one 8x8 matrix with 16-bit elements

When `.num` = `.x2`, the elements of the second matrix are storedd from the next source register
in each thread as per the layout in above table. Similarly, when `.num` = `.x4`, elements of the
third and fourth matrices are stored from the subsequent source registers in each thread.

For 16x8 matrix shape, each of the 32 threads in the warp provides four elements of data per matrix.

Each element in the source operand `r` is of type `.b32` and contains four 8 bit elements `e0`,
`e1`, `e2`, `e3` with `e0` and `e3` containing the LSB and MSB respectively of register `r`.





[Figure: mma-stmatrix-fragments-168]
STMATRIX FRAGMENT LAYOUT: 16x8 MATRIX WITH 8-BIT ELEMENTS

=== MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE ===
Matrix size: 16 rows x 8 columns
Element type: 8-bit elements (integer, e.g., .u8 or .s8)
Total elements: 128
This is used with stmatrix instruction for storing matrix fragments to shared memory.

=== GRID STRUCTURE ===
Rows: 16 matrix rows (0 through 15)
Columns: 8 matrix columns (0 through 7)
Threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads total)
Registers per thread used: 2 registers (each register holds 4 bytes = four 8-bit elements, elements e0/e1 in first register, e2/e3 in second register)

=== ELEMENT PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS ===
Each 32-bit register holds 4 eight-bit elements packed together.
Register 0 (r0): holds elements e0, e1, e2, e3 — but the layout splits them:
  e0 and e1 appear in matrix rows 0-7 (upper half)
  e2 and e3 appear in matrix rows 8-15 (lower half)
So effectively each thread contributes 2 elements to the upper 8 rows and 2 elements to the lower 8 rows, using what appears to be 2 registers total (one register covering e0+e1, another covering e2+e3), or alternatively 4 elements packed in a single register with e0,e1,e2,e3 as byte lanes.

=== COMPLETE ELEMENT-BY-ELEMENT LAYOUT TABLE ===

Row 0:  Col0=T0:e0,  Col1=T0:e1,  Col2=T1:e0,  Col3=T1:e1,  Col4=T2:e0,  Col5=T2:e1,  Col6=T3:e0,  Col7=T3:e1
Row 1:  Col0=T4:e0,  Col1=T4:e1,  Col2=T5:e0,  Col3=T5:e1,  Col4=T6:e0,  Col5=T6:e1,  Col6=T7:e0,  Col7=T7:e1
Row 2:  Col0=T8:e0,  Col1=T8:e1,  Col2=T9:e0,  Col3=T9:e1,  Col4=T10:e0, Col5=T10:e1, Col6=T11:e0, Col7=T11:e1
Row 3:  Col0=T12:e0, Col1=T12:e1, Col2=T13:e0, Col3=T13:e1, Col4=T14:e0, Col5=T14:e1, Col6=T15:e0, Col7=T15:e1
Row 4:  Col0=T16:e0, Col1=T16:e1, Col2=T17:e0, Col3=T17:e1, Col4=T18:e0, Col5=T18:e1, Col6=T19:e0, Col7=T19:e1
Row 5:  Col0=T20:e0, Col1=T20:e1, Col2=T21:e0, Col3=T21:e1, Col4=T22:e0, Col5=T22:e1, Col6=T23:e0, Col7=T23:e1
Row 6:  Col0=T24:e0, Col1=T24:e1, Col2=T25:e0, Col3=T25:e1, Col4=T26:e0, Col5=T26:e1, Col6=T27:e0, Col7=T27:e1
Row 7:  Col0=T28:e0, Col1=T28:e1, Col2=T29:e0, Col3=T29:e1, Col4=T30:e0, Col5=T30:e1, Col6=T31:e0, Col7=T31:e1
Row 8:  Col0=T0:e2,  Col1=T0:e3,  Col2=T1:e2,  Col3=T1:e3,  Col4=T2:e2,  Col5=T2:e3,  Col6=T3:e2,  Col7=T3:e3
Row 9:  Col0=T4:e2,  Col1=T4:e3,  Col2=T5:e2,  Col3=T5:e3,  Col4=T6:e2,  Col5=T6:e3,  Col6=T7:e2,  Col7=T7:e3
Row 10: Col0=T8:e2,  Col1=T8:e3,  Col2=T9:e2,  Col3=T9:e3,  Col4=T10:e2, Col5=T10:e3, Col6=T11:e2, Col7=T11:e3
Row 11: Col0=T12:e2, Col1=T12:e3, Col2=T13:e2, Col3=T13:e3, Col4=T14:e2, Col5=T14:e3, Col6=T15:e2, Col7=T15:e3
Row 12: Col0=T16:e2, Col1=T16:e3, Col2=T17:e2, Col3=T17:e3, Col4=T18:e2, Col5=T18:e3, Col6=T19:e2, Col7=T19:e3
Row 13: Col0=T20:e2, Col1=T20:e3, Col2=T21:e2, Col3=T21:e3, Col4=T22:e2, Col5=T22:e3, Col6=T23:e2, Col7=T23:e3
Row 14: Col0=T24:e2, Col1=T24:e3, Col2=T25:e2, Col3=T25:e3, Col4=T26:e2, Col5=T26:e3, Col6=T27:e2, Col7=T27:e3
Row 15: Col0=T28:e2, Col1=T28:e3, Col2=T29:e2, Col3=T29:e3, Col4=T30:e2, Col5=T30:e3, Col6=T31:e2, Col7=T31:e3

=== THREAD GROUPING PATTERNS ===

UPPER HALF (rows 0-7), elements e0 and e1:
  Row 0: T0 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T1 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T2 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T3 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 1: T4 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T5 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T6 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T7 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 2: T8 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T9 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T10 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T11 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 3: T12 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T13 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T14 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T15 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 4: T16 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T17 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T18 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T19 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 5: T20 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T21 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T22 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T23 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 6: T24 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T25 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T26 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T27 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 7: T28 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T29 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T30 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T31 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7

LOWER HALF (rows 8-15), elements e2 and e3:
  Row 8:  T0 (e2,e3) covers cols 0-1; T1 (e2,e3) covers cols 2-3; T2 (e2,e3) covers cols 4-5; T3 (e2,e3) covers cols 6-7
  Row 9:  T4 (e2,e3) covers cols 0-1; T5 (e2,e3) covers cols 2-3; T6 (e2,e3) covers cols 4-5; T7 (e2,e3) covers cols 6-7
  Row 10: T8 (e2,e3) covers cols 0-1; T9 (e2,e3) covers cols 2-3; T

STMATRIX FRAGMENT LAYOUT: 16x8 MATRIX WITH 8-BIT ELEMENTS

=== MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE ===
Matrix size: 16 rows x 8 columns
Element type: 8-bit elements (integer, e.g., .u8 or .s8)
Total elements: 128
This is used with stmatrix instruction for storing matrix fragments to shared memory.

=== GRID STRUCTURE ===
Rows: 16 matrix rows (0 through 15)
Columns: 8 matrix columns (0 through 7)
Threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads total)
Registers per thread used: 2 registers (each register holds 4 bytes = four 8-bit elements, elements e0/e1 in first register, e2/e3 in second register)

=== ELEMENT PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS ===
Each 32-bit register holds 4 eight-bit elements packed together.
Register 0 (r0): holds elements e0, e1, e2, e3 — but the layout splits them:
  e0 and e1 appear in matrix rows 0-7 (upper half)
  e2 and e3 appear in matrix rows 8-15 (lower half)
So effectively each thread contributes 2 elements to the upper 8 rows and 2 elements to the lower 8 rows, using what appears to be 2 registers total (one register covering e0+e1, another covering e2+e3), or alternatively 4 elements packed in a single register with e0,e1,e2,e3 as byte lanes.

=== COMPLETE ELEMENT-BY-ELEMENT LAYOUT TABLE ===

Row 0:  Col0=T0:e0,  Col1=T0:e1,  Col2=T1:e0,  Col3=T1:e1,  Col4=T2:e0,  Col5=T2:e1,  Col6=T3:e0,  Col7=T3:e1
Row 1:  Col0=T4:e0,  Col1=T4:e1,  Col2=T5:e0,  Col3=T5:e1,  Col4=T6:e0,  Col5=T6:e1,  Col6=T7:e0,  Col7=T7:e1
Row 2:  Col0=T8:e0,  Col1=T8:e1,  Col2=T9:e0,  Col3=T9:e1,  Col4=T10:e0, Col5=T10:e1, Col6=T11:e0, Col7=T11:e1
Row 3:  Col0=T12:e0, Col1=T12:e1, Col2=T13:e0, Col3=T13:e1, Col4=T14:e0, Col5=T14:e1, Col6=T15:e0, Col7=T15:e1
Row 4:  Col0=T16:e0, Col1=T16:e1, Col2=T17:e0, Col3=T17:e1, Col4=T18:e0, Col5=T18:e1, Col6=T19:e0, Col7=T19:e1
Row 5:  Col0=T20:e0, Col1=T20:e1, Col2=T21:e0, Col3=T21:e1, Col4=T22:e0, Col5=T22:e1, Col6=T23:e0, Col7=T23:e1
Row 6:  Col0=T24:e0, Col1=T24:e1, Col2=T25:e0, Col3=T25:e1, Col4=T26:e0, Col5=T26:e1, Col6=T27:e0, Col7=T27:e1
Row 7:  Col0=T28:e0, Col1=T28:e1, Col2=T29:e0, Col3=T29:e1, Col4=T30:e0, Col5=T30:e1, Col6=T31:e0, Col7=T31:e1

Row 11: Col0=T12:e2, Col1=T12:e3, Col2=T13:e2, Col3=T13:e3, Col4=T14:e2, Col5=T14:e3, Col6=T15:e2, Col7=T15:e3

=== THREAD GROUPING PATTERNS ===

UPPER HALF (rows 0-7), elements e0 and e1:
  Row 0: T0 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T1 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T2 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T3 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 1: T4 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T5 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T6 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T7 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 2: T8 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T9 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T10 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T11 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 3: T12 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T13 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T14 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T15 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 4: T16 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T17 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T18 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T19 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 5: T20 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T21 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T22 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T23 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 6: T24 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T25 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T26 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T27 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7
  Row 7: T28 (e0,e1) covers cols 0-1; T29 (e0,e1) covers cols 2-3; T30 (e0,e1) covers cols 4-5; T31 (e0,e1) covers cols 6-7

LOWER HALF (rows 8-15), elements e2 and e3:
  Row 8:  T0 (e2,e3) covers cols 0-1; T1 (e2,e3) covers cols 2-3; T2 (e2,e3) covers cols 4-5; T3 (e2,e3) covers cols 6-7
  Row 9:  T4 (e2,e3) covers cols 0-1; T5 (e2,e3) covers cols 2-3; T6 (e2,e3) covers cols 4-5; T7 (e2,e3) covers cols 6-7
  Row 10: T8 (e2,e3) covers cols 0-1; T9 (e2,e3) covers cols 2-3; T


Figure 108 stmatrix fragment layout for one 16x8 matrix with 8 bit elements

Optional qualifier `.trans` indicates that the matrix is stored in column-major format. However,
for 16x8 matrices, `.trans` is mandatory.

The `stmatrix` instruction is treated as a weak memory operation in the [Memory Consistency Model](#memory-consistency-model).

PTX ISA Notes

Introduced in PTX ISA version 7.8.

Support for `.m16n8` shape is introduced in PTX ISA version 8.6.

Support for `.b8` type with `stmatrix` is introduced in PTX ISA version 8.6.

Target ISA Notes

Requires `sm_90` or higher.

Shape `.m16n8` is supported on following architectures:

* `sm_100a`
* `sm_101a` (Renamed to `sm_110a` from PTX ISA version 9.0)
* `sm_120a`
* And is supported on following family-specific architectures from PTX ISA version 8.8:

  > + `sm_100f` or higher in the same family
  > + `sm_101f` or higher in the same family (Renamed to `sm_110f` from PTX ISA version 9.0)
  > + `sm_120f` or higher in the same family
* `sm_110f` or higher in the same family

Type `.b8` with `stmatrix` is supported on following architectures:

* `sm_100a`
* `sm_101a` (Renamed to `sm_110a` from PTX ISA version 9.0)
* `sm_120a`
* And is supported on following family-specific architectures from PTX ISA version 8.8:

  > + `sm_100f` or higher in the same family
  > + `sm_101f` or higher in the same family (Renamed to `sm_110f` from PTX ISA version 9.0)
  > + `sm_120f` or higher in the same family
* `sm_110f` or higher in the same family

Examples

```
// Store a single 8x8 matrix using 64-bit addressing
.reg .b64 addr;
.reg .b32 r;
stmatrix.sync.aligned.m8n8.x1.shared.b16 [addr], {r};

// Store two 8x8 matrices in column-major format
.reg .b64 addr;
.reg .b32 r<2>;
stmatrix.sync.aligned.m8n8.x2.trans.shared::cta.b16 [addr], {r0, r1};

// Store four 8x8 matrices
.reg .b64 addr;
.reg .b32 r<4>;
stmatrix.sync.aligned.m8n8.x4.b16 [addr], {r0, r1, r2, r3};

// Store a single 16x8 matrix using generic addressing
.reg .b64 addr;
.reg .b32 r;
stmatrix.sync.aligned.m16n8.x1.trans.shared.b8 [addr], {r};

// Store two 16x8 matrices
.reg .b64 addr;
.reg .b32 r<2>;
stmatrix.sync.aligned.m16n8.x2.trans.shared::cta.b8 [addr],{r0, r1};

// Store four 16x8 matrices
.reg .b64 addr;
.reg .b32 r<4>;
stmatrix.sync.aligned.m16n8.x4.b8 [addr], {r0, r1, r2, r3};
```
